message:
fix(reactor): Interest NOT mask, deregister retry semantics, modify lock safety, 64-bit guard

interest.rs:
- Add Interest::ALL constant enumerating all currently defined interest flags
  (READABLE | WRITABLE | ERROR | HUP | PRIORITY | ONESHOT | EDGE_TRIGGERED).
  This provides a canonical bitmask for operations that need to stay within
  the defined flag space
- Fix Not impl: the bare bitwise complement `!self.0` set all upper bits
  (bits 7-31) which are undefined and could propagate into epoll/kqueue calls
  as unintended flags. Now masks with ALL.0 so only defined flags survive
  complementation
- Add tests: not_masks_undefined_bits (inverting unknown bit yields only
  defined bits), not_none_equals_all (complementing NONE yields ALL),
  and augment existing bit_operators test with defined-bit assertion

epoll.rs:
- Fix modify() lock lifetime: the match block returned results while still
  holding `state` lock. In the EBADF cleanup path, the `return Err(...)` inside
  the `if !fd_still_valid` block returned while the lock was still held, but the
  fall-through `Err(err)` after the block also held the lock. Restructured to:
  1. Assign match result to a binding
  2. Explicit `drop(state)` after the match
  3. Return result after lock is released
- Fix EBADF branch logic inversion: the `if !fd_still_valid` block cleaned up
  stale state then returned, but the fall-through `Err(err)` ran when the fd WAS
  still valid (which is the unexpected case). Flipped to `if fd_still_valid {
  Err(err) } else { cleanup + Err(NotFound) }` for correct semantics

registration.rs:
- Fix explicit deregister retry semantics: when the first deregister attempt
  fails and the retry succeeds (or returns NotFound — meaning another path
  already cleaned it up), the explicit deregister should return Ok(()) since the
  registration IS now removed. Previously it always returned the first error
  even when retry succeeded, causing callers to believe deregistration failed
  when it actually worked
- Add AlwaysFailReactor test mock (both attempts always fail) alongside existing
  FlakyReactor (first fails, second succeeds) to test both outcomes
- Rename test to explicit_deregister_transient_error_recovers_and_returns_ok
- Add explicit_deregister_persistent_error_returns_err_after_retry test

token.rs:
- Add compile_error! for non-64-bit targets: SlabToken encodes two u32 fields
  (index + generation) into a single usize. On 32-bit targets this would
  silently truncate, producing duplicate tokens. A compile-time error is
  preferable to silent corruption

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
